title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 20 lines).
summary_text: 1. High: [auto-push-after-commit.sh:36](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/auto-push-after-commit.sh#L36) auto-pushes on any detected `git commit` without explicit user intent. That is an external publication side effect in a hook, and it uses the hook’s current repo, not the repo from the original command parsed at [auto-push-after-commit.sh:16](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/hooks/auto-push-after-commit.sh#L16), so `git -C other/repo commit` can push the wrong repository. 2. High: [gha-node24-migrate.sh:132](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/scripts/gha-node24-migrate.sh#L132), [gha-node24-migrate.sh:133](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/scripts/gha-node24-migrate.sh#L133), and [gha-node24-migrate.sh:134](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/scripts/gha-node24-migrate.sh#L134) swallow `stash`/`pull --rebase` failures with `|| true`, then [gha-node24-migrate.sh:136](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/scripts/gha-node24-migrate.sh#L136)-[gha-node24-migrate.sh:148](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/scripts/gha-node24-migrate.sh#L148) still commit and push. A conflict or failed reapply can leave a dirty/conflicted tree that gets committed anyway. 3. Medium: [eval-skill.sh:350](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh#L350)-[eval-skill.sh:357](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh#L357) now drops valid env-prefixed commands when the assignment uses quotes or command substitution, e.g. `FOO="x" cmd` or `FOO=$(cat f) cmd`. That weakens the phantom-command check by skipping real commands entirely. 4. Medium: [eval-skill.sh:337](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh#L337) and [eval-skill.sh:410](/Users/masayuki/Dev/claude-config/assets/skills/research-loop/eval-skill.sh#L410) no longer filter CJK/prose lines inside tagged shell blocks before `command -v`. The previous implementation did, so Japanese explanatory lines in ```bash``` blocks will now be miscounted as phantom commands. `bash -n` and `node --check` passed on the touched scripts; I did not run behavioral tests.
